Title: Verify system architecture
Test Steps:
1. Identify the components: Data display, Local user, Data organizer, and Firebase.
2. Verify the relationships between components.
3. Confirm that the local user interacts with the data organizer, which then communicates with Firebase for data management and synchronization.
4. Verify that the data display component retrieves data from the data organizer.
Expected Result: All components are correctly identified, and their relationships are as described in the UML diagram.

Title: Verify database schema
Test Steps:
1. Identify the classes: Chat, Message, Relation, User, Post, and Fridge.
2. Verify the relationships between classes.
3. Confirm that a chat has many messages, a user has many relations, and a fridge has many posts.
Expected Result: All classes are correctly identified, and their relationships are as described in the UML diagram.

Title: Verify sequence diagram for removing a product
Preconditions: A product exists in the fridge.
Test Data: A product to be removed from the fridge.
Test Steps:
1. Verify that the main schema changes the fragment to the fridge fragment.
2. Verify that the fridge fragment sends a request to remove the product to the Firebase database.
Expected Result: The product is successfully removed from the Firebase database.

Title: Verify chat creation and messaging
Preconditions: Two users are registered in the system.
Test Data: Two users and a chat message.
Test Steps:
1. Verify that a new chat is created between the two users.
2. Verify that a new message is sent and received within the chat.
Expected Result: The chat is created, and the message is successfully sent and received between the two users.

Title: Verify user registration and authentication
Test Data: A new user with valid credentials.
Test Steps:
1. Verify that a new user is successfully registered in the system.
2. Verify that the user can log in with the provided credentials.
Expected Result: The user is successfully registered and can log in with the provided credentials.

Title: Verify fridge creation and management
Preconditions: A user is registered in the system.
Test Data: A new fridge and products to add to the fridge.
Test Steps:
1. Verify that a new fridge is created for the user.
2. Verify that products can be added to the fridge.
3. Verify that products can be removed from the fridge.
Expected Result: The fridge is created, and products can be added and removed as expected.

Title: Verify post creation and management
Preconditions: A user is registered in the system and has a fridge.
Test Data: A new post related to a fridge item.
Test Steps:
1. Verify that a new post is created for the user's fridge.
2. Verify that the post can be edited or deleted.
Expected Result: The post is created, and it can be edited or deleted as expected.

Title: Verify user relations
Preconditions: Two users are registered in the system.
Test Data: Two users to establish a relation between.
Test Steps:
1. Verify that users can establish a relation with each other.
2. Verify that users can view their relations.
Expected Result: Users can establish and view their relations as expected.

Title: Verify edge cases and error handling
Test Data: Invalid inputs, missing data, and unexpected user actions.
Test Steps:
1. Test the system with invalid inputs, missing data, and unexpected user actions.
2. Verify that the system handles these cases appropriately, displaying error messages or taking corrective actions.
Expected Result: The system handles edge cases and errors appropriately, providing clear error messages or taking corrective actions as needed.
